verdict: INCONCLUSIVE_DATA_PENDING — no decomposition channel loaded; missing: []
falsification_rule_text: The hypothesis is SUPPORTED if UK labour-share decline 1979-1990 exceeds counterfactual sectoral-composition-only projection by at least 3 percentage points, AND if event-study estimates around the three reform dates (1980 Right-to-Buy, 1984 union-law, 1986 Big Bang) jointly explain at least half of the residual. REFUTED if sectoral composition alone explains >80% of the decline, or if the reform-window event-study coefficients are statistically indistinguishable from zero.
falsification_test_text: UK 1979-1990 shift-share decomposition of labour-share decline into sector-mix vs within-sector components, plus event studies (±3y) around 1980, 1984, 1986 reform dates. Refute if sector-mix explains >80% OR event coefficients indistinguishable from zero at p<0.10.

variables_missing.name: thatcher_reform_bundle_indicator
variables_missing.source: constructed: indicators for 1980 Right-to-Buy enactment, 1980/82/84/88 Employment Acts (union-law tightening), 1986 Big Bang financial deregulation; treated as joint event-study.
